title: simsia molinae
intro: Simsia molinae, also known as the Guayabillo or Yellowflower Simsia, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to Mexico and parts of Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its essential oil and flavonoids. Simsia molinae is believed to provide several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Simsia molinae include infusions, decoctions, and essential oils, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
